query: What are the top AI news headlines today? Include sources.
textResponse:
Here are some of the **top AI-related headlines circulating today, Thursday, April 9, 2026**, based on recent widely covered developments I could verify online:

1. **OpenAI and Google employees back Anthropic in Pentagon lawsuit** — A notable policy/industry story: employees from OpenAI and Google DeepMind filed in support of Anthropic after the U.S. Defense Department labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk tied to disputes over military/surveillance use of AI. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/openai-and-google-employees-rush-to-anthropics-defense-in-dod-lawsuit/?utm_source=openai))

2. **Microsoft pushes broader enterprise AI strategy with new “Frontier Suite”** — Microsoft recently announced a major expansion of its enterprise AI offerings, including broader model choice and general availability plans for Agent 365 and Microsoft 365 E7. This remains one of the bigger AI-business stories still shaping today’s coverage. ([blogs.microsoft.com](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/09/introducing-the-first-frontier-suite-built-on-intelligence-trust/?utm_source=openai))

3. **AI agent standards continue gaining momentum** — The industry shift toward common standards for AI agents remains a major theme, with OpenAI, Anthropic, and others having joined a Linux Foundation effort around interoperable agent infrastructure. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/09/openai-anthropic-and-block-join-new-linux-foundation-effort-to-standardize-the-ai-agent-era/?utm_source=openai))

4. **Big Tech competition in AI infrastructure remains front and center** — Microsoft, Meta, OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic are still in an infrastructure and platform race, including enterprise distribution, agent tooling, and large-scale compute buildouts. Recent reporting and company announcements keep this among the most important AI storylines. ([blogs.microsoft.com](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/09/introducing-the-first-frontier-suite-built-on-intelligence-trust/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Government use of frontier AI is becoming a defining policy battleground** — Beyond product launches, one of today’s biggest AI themes is whether frontier model providers will support military, surveillance, and other sensitive government uses, with the Anthropic case serving as a focal point. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/openai-and-google-employees-rush-to-anthropics-defense-in-dod-lawsuit/?utm_source=openai))

**Sources**
- TechCrunch on the Anthropic/DoD lawsuit and employee support. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/09/openai-and-google-employees-rush-to-anthropics-defense-in-dod-lawsuit/?utm_source=openai))
- Microsoft official blog on its Frontier Suite and Agent 365 announcements. ([blogs.microsoft.com](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/09/introducing-the-first-frontier-suite-built-on-intelligence-trust/?utm_source=openai))
- TechCrunch on the Linux Foundation’s agentic AI standards effort. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/09/openai-anthropic-and-block-join-new-linux-foundation-effort-to-standardize-the-ai-agent-era/?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also turn this into a **more traditional news digest format** with:
- **headline + 1-sentence summary + source**, or
- a version limited to **today’s freshest articles from Reuters/AP/TechCrunch/The Verge only**.
